summary: Backend-only Task 6 testing completed. Core baseline/runner flows pass, but StaticShiftAgent commit logic violates the selected '2 consistent inferences' rule under mixed inference sequences.

critical.issue: StaticShiftAgent commits shift after cumulative counts (e.g., inferred shifts 2,1,2) instead of requiring two consistent consecutive inferences; this can cause premature lock-in.

action_items: Update StaticShiftAgent.observe() commit logic to require two consecutive same inferred shifts (not cumulative frequency).
After fix, rerun full pytest suite and confirm the new regression test passes.
Optionally add explicit unit test coverage for consecutive vs non-consecutive inference behavior in existing test_agents.py.
critical_code_review_comments: RandomAgent correctly uses local random.Random(seed) and is deterministic after reset().
run_episode() integration is correct for single-episode execution and summary stats computation.
StaticShiftAgent currently ignores temporal consistency and commits based on total count per inferred shift, which is risky under noisy feedback patterns.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Base suite remains green (40/40). Added /app/tests/test_static_shift_commit_rule_regression.py to verify non-consecutive inferences do not trigger commit. Current code fails this case, indicating commit condition mismatch with selected option-b semantics.
rca of the issue: RCA: observe() tracks inferred shifts in _shift_counts and commits when any shift count reaches threshold. This is cumulative across the whole episode, not consistency in sequence. Repro: infer shifts [2, 1, 2] with required_consistent_inferences=2; agent commits to 2 after third inference although the sequence is not consistently 2. Expected with option-b intent: no commit until two consecutive matching inferences. NO MOCKED APIs used.
